summary: Matched major HSD GObj baselib code by replacing gobjplink assembly with C, removing the gobjgxlink assembly object in favor of the existing C object, and moving a large block of gobj runtime/render/user-object logic from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.s into src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.c. The PR also strengthened gobj headers with concrete struct layouts, renamed several global data symbols, added missing gx-link/plink prototypes, and updated obj_files.mk to build the new C sources.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjplink.s and added src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjplink.c with C implementations for process-link ordering, GObj creation, GObj removal, and GObj relinking/reprioritization logic.

Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.s from the build and expanded src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c with matched C for gx-link removal, gx-link priority relinking, and reorder-by-other-gobj behavior.

Moved a large set of functions out of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.s into src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.c, including proc-running logic, render traversal, camera/render callback helpers, HSD object release helpers, and small GObj function-list helpers.
Updated obj_files.mk so gobjplink builds from src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjplink.c.o and gobjgxlink builds from src/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjgxlink.c.o instead of the deleted assembly files.

Reworked include/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h to give HSD_GObj and HSD_GObjProc explicit offset-commented layouts, add HSD_GOBJ_GXLINK_NONE as 0xFF, and expose HSD_GObjLibInitDataType, gobj list globals, plinklow_gobjs, and newly needed prototypes.
Renamed important globals in remaining assembly/data from anonymous labels to semantic names: HSD_GObjLibInitData, gobj_alloc_data, gobjproc_alloc_data, and plinklow_gobjs.
Replaced local externs and raw __assert calls in gobjgxlink.c/gobjuserdata.c with shared header declarations and assert_line forms.
Removed a redundant local extern for func_80390EB8 from src/melee/ft/ftdrawcommon.c after adding the declaration to the gobj header.
smart_moves: Kept the C close to the assembly control flow while adding semantic helper comments such as GObj_RunProcs, GObj_SetTextureCamera, and GObj_RunGXLinkMaxCallbacks, making future naming work easier without overclaiming final names.
Modeled HSD_GObjProc flags as bitfields at byte 0x0D, matching the assembly's rlwinm/rlwimi-style flag access and allowing cleaner C for flags_1, flags_2, and flags_3.
Changed HSD_GObjLibInitData.unk_2 from u32* to u64*, matching the code that tests p_link masks with 64-bit shifts.
Used u64 gxlink_prios and simple shifting loops in render traversal to match the original __shr2u/__shl2i-style 64-bit priority-mask behavior.
Introduced small inline helpers for list insertion and render-callback save/restore patterns, which preserved repeated assembly idioms while keeping source readable.
Used #pragma dont_inline around the HSD_JObjDispAll call path to prevent func_80390EB8 from being inlined, explicitly documenting a matching-sensitive file-boundary/codegen concern.
Centralized previously local externs in gobj.h/gobjgxlink.h, reducing duplicated declarations and making the matched C usable by neighboring files.
Named data globals in the remaining assembly instead of leaving only lbl_* labels, improving cross-file readability while leaving not-yet-decompiled assembly intact.
decomp_lessons: For HSD GObj work, explicit offset comments in struct headers are valuable because many functions are list-manipulation heavy and depend on exact field offsets such as next/prev, next_gx/prev_gx, proc, render_cb, gxlink_prios, hsd_obj, and user_data fields.
Bitfields can be the right tool for matching packed byte flags when the assembly uses bit inserts/extracts; here HSD_GObjProc flags_1, flags_2, and flags_3 occupy byte 0x0D.
Use named sentinel constants for magic link values: HSD_GOBJ_GXLINK_NONE is 0xFF and HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE remains 0xFF.

GObj p-link and gx-link lists have separate head/tail arrays and different ordering fields; p-link uses next/prev plus p_priority and plinklow_gobjs, while gx-link uses next_gx/prev_gx plus render_priority and lbl_804D7820/lbl_804D7824.

When removing or moving the currently running GObj/proc, defer actions through a global pending-action structure if the assembly shows current-object guards; this PR modeled lbl_804CE3E4 as a flags/type/link/priority/gobj carrier.
Header promotion can unblock multiple matches: adding prototypes and extern globals in gobj.h allowed removal of ad hoc externs from fighter draw and gobjgxlink/gobjuserdata code.
assert_line is the preferred project-side wrapper for preserving file/line assertion behavior without carrying raw __assert string externs in each source file.
Keep unmatched assembly and matched C synchronized through obj_files.mk, and rename remaining assembly data symbols when C code now references them semantically.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_* names for baselib object types and globals when evidence supports them: HSD_GObj, HSD_GObjProc, HSD_GObjLibInitData, HSD_GObjLibInitDataType.
Use descriptive lowercase names for allocator/list globals exposed from data: gobj_alloc_data, gobjproc_alloc_data, plinklow_gobjs.
Retain func_8039xxxx names for functions that are matched but not conclusively named; add nearby semantic comments only when the behavior is clear.
Use HSD_GOBJ_* macros for GObj sentinel and classifier constants, including HSD_GOBJ_GXLINK_NONE and existing classifier names.
Prefer pointer style and offset comments consistent with the updated header: `HSD_GObj*` and `/* 0xNN */` field annotations.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced entire assembly translation units with C only after corresponding obj_files.mk entries were switched from asm .s.o to src .c.o.
Preserved matching-sensitive codegen using inline helper functions for recurring list operations and `#pragma dont_inline on` for the func_80390EB8 call site.
Represented 64-bit masks as u64 and used `1LL << gobj->p_link` plus shifting loops to align with assembly that called 64-bit shift helpers.
Used temporary saved-global variables around render callbacks to mirror assembly patterns that store current gobj/proc globals, call through a function pointer, then restore the previous value.
Matched list splice operations by explicitly updating both predecessor and successor pointers and head/tail arrays in the same branch structure visible in assembly.
Kept some assembly data in gobj.s but renamed labels there so newly matched C could reference stable semantic global names.

review_feedback.notes: No textual review comments were present in the slice; only an approval review is recorded.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename func_80390CFC, func_803901F0, func_80390228, func_8039032C, func_8039084C, func_80390908, or func_803909D8 to final HSD GObj API names.
Search for later cleanup of lbl_804CE3E4 and the _unk_gobj_struct pending-action flags structure.
Search for PRs that fully decompile the remaining asm/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.s functions after this partial gobj.c match.
Search for changes to HSD_GObjLibInitDataType.unk_1 and unk_2 to determine final field names and semantics.
Search for later header normalization of HSD_GObjList and lbl_804D7820/lbl_804D7824/lbl_804D782C names.
